title: lastrea hirtipes
intro: Lastrea hirtipes, also known as the hirsute lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Athyriaceae. It is native to various regions, including Asia and the Pacific Islands, and is characterized by its distinctive hirsute or hairy appearance. The medicinal properties of Lastrea hirtipes include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote overall health and well-being, with reported benefits including improved digestion, reduced stress, and enhanced immune function.
